RT @anmccartney: DataProofer — the proofreader for spreadsheets — is live! Check it out @ https://t.co/WON5vGbuVk 🎉Congrats @gerald_arthur…
@_BrianByrne Really curious what you think because I literally said "let's build this for Brian" a lot
All of this is to say, let us know how we can improve it. We want this tool to serve our community. We want you to make it your own.
Datasets will continue to get larger and larger, and sometimes finding the story is daunting. I hope that this helps fight that.
I hope journalists feel more confident tackling huge data pieces, knowing it won't be weeks of manual spot-checking. That's a big win.
If we can take some off the proofreading workload off the backs of analysts, vizzers, etc- I hope it frees them up to be more creative.

So when people say "Can @dataproofer [X]" - we can say, "it will tomorrow". It's that easy. 

Duplicate a test that's close, modify it, boom
Part of the reason I'm so excited for folks to look at it, is that we made it easy to add new things. The feedback will decide what.
We made a tool that will make your job not only easier, but I think a little bit fun. It's the foundation, meant to be built on.

RT @dataproofer: You can download the Dataproofer app here: https://t.co/OH8aoQR2BU

And read more or check out the code here: https://t.co…
